title: Broken Pipe
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Running container fails with `write |1: broken pipe` ; possible duplicate of https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1553

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ sudo podman run -i -t --log-level=debug alpine

DEBU[0000] Using graph root /var/lib/containers/storage

DEBU[0000] Using volume path /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "btrfs"

INFO[0000] Found CNI network podman (type=bridge) at /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist

                                                       level=debug msg="Created veth pair" subsys=endpoint-connector vethPair="[tmp54f66 lxc8f8b1ccbf241]"
                                                                                                                                                          level=debug msg="Configuring link" interface=eth0 ipAddr=10.252.129.37 netLink="&{LinkAttrs:{Index:41 MTU:9000 TxQLen:0 Name:eth0 HardwareAddr:b6:3b:70:60:a5:b3 Flags:broadcast|multicast RawFlags:4098 ParentIndex:42 MasterIndex:0 Namespace:<nil> Alias: Statistics:0xc0006d6108 Promisc:0 Xdp:0xc0004fd240 EncapType:ether Protinfo:<nil> OperState:down NetNsID:0 NumTxQueues:0 NumRxQueues:0 Vfs:[]} PeerName:}" subsys=cilium-cni

                                                                                                             level=debug msg="Adding route" route="{Prefix:{IP:0.0.0.0 Mask:00000000} Nexthop:10.252.0.1 Local:<nil> Device: MTU:8950 Proto:0 Scope:0 Table:0 Type:0}" subsys=cilium-cni

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

```

comments.body:
Strongly suspect this is related to the CNI plugin in use

On Wed, Mar 20, 2019, 09:55 Lokesh Mandvekar <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> @kbaegis <https://github.com/kbaegis> I'm guessing ubuntu 18.04?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2718#issuecomment-474839800>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCFRBcLCW8x_Af5agum1i5zHi11S2ks5vYj3mgaJpZM4b_PDg>
> .
>
We're not even getting to Conmon - I'm almost certain this is a CNI failure. Can you try a `--net=host` container to be sure of this?

On Wed, Mar 20, 2019 at 10:22:07AM -0700, Matthew Heon wrote:
> We're not even getting to Conmon - I'm almost certain this is a CNI failure. Can you try a `--net=host` container to be sure of this?
> 

ack, cni plugins are an older version right now on ubuntu. I'll update to the
latest today.
-- 
Lokesh
IRC, GitHub: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5

The report mentions a Gentoo kernel, so I'm not certain this is the PPA
we're talking about

On Wed, Mar 20, 2019, 13:45 Lokesh Mandvekar <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> On Wed, Mar 20, 2019 at 10:22:07AM -0700, Matthew Heon wrote:
> > We're not even getting to Conmon - I'm almost certain this is a CNI
> failure. Can you try a `--net=host` container to be sure of this?
> >
>
> ack, cni plugins are an older version right now on ubuntu. I'll update to
> the
> latest today.
> --
> Lokesh
> IRC, GitHub: lsm5
> GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
> https://keybase.io/lsm5
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2718#issuecomment-474953525>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCPb85c6M5FB2tJBZu-A-hukqNUDGks5vYnPIgaJpZM4b_PDg>
> .
>

Original error: `ERRO[0006] error parsing CNI plugin result "IP4:{IP:{IP:10.3.85.4 Mask:ffffffff} Gateway:10.3.0.1 Routes:[{Dst:{IP:10.3.0.1 Mask:ffffffff} GW:<nil>} {Dst:{IP:0.0.0.0 Mask:00000000} GW:10.3.0.1}]}, DNS:{Nameservers:[] Domain: Search:[] Options:[]}": cannot convert version ["" "0.1.0" "0.2.0"] to 0.4.0: cannot convert version ["" "0.1.0" "0.2.0"] to 0.4.0` 

After injecting `    "cniVersion": "0.3.1",` as a workaround into the appropriate location (/etc/cni/net.d/05-cilium.conf), that is the resulting error.

```
$ sudo podman run -d --log-level=debug --net=host alpine

```
@kbaegis @lsm5 Is this still an issue?  Seems like a packaging problem.
@rhatdan I think you’re right. Runc wasn’t built with apparmor support but libpod was.
